---
title: "Small-seeded panicgrass (Dichanthelium chamaelonche)"
slug: "dichanthelium-chamaelonche"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180826182/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Small-seeded panicgrass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/180826182/medium.jpg" alt="Small-seeded panicgrass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damonmoore">
damonmoore</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107506535">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=956624)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing grass characterized by its delicate stems, narrow leaves, and small, inconspicuous seed heads. In Florida, it is found in sandy, open habitats such as flatwoods, scrub, and pine barrens. It grows primarily during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover in native plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/956624-Dichanthelium-chamaelonche)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/27)
